terms.definition: A precise list of instructions written for a computer to follow, step by step.
Human-readable text that describes a program. In Go, files end in .go.
A named place in memory that holds a value you can read or change later.
What kind of value something is — e.g. string for text, int for whole numbers.
A named block of instructions. func main() is where a runnable program starts.
Runs one branch of code only when a condition is true; otherwise skips or runs else.
Repeats a block of code. Go only has the for keyword, which can act as a for, while, or range loop.
A dynamically-sized, flexible view into elements of an array. The most common way to work with sequences in Go.
An unordered collection of key-value pairs. Also called a dictionary or hash table in other languages.
A composite type that groups together fields (like a lightweight class without methods).
A value that holds the memory address of another value. Use & to get an address and * to follow it.
A function attached to a type via a receiver parameter. Called with dot notation: value.Method().
A set of method signatures. Any type that implements all those methods satisfies the interface automatically — no explicit declaration needed.
A lightweight thread managed by the Go runtime. Start one by adding go before a function call.
A typed conduit for sending and receiving values between goroutines. Created with make(chan Type).
Schedules a function call to run just before the surrounding function returns. Used for cleanup like closing files.
The built-in interface representing an error condition. Functions that can fail return an error as their last value.
A collection of Go files in the same directory. Every Go file belongs to a package, declared at the top with package <name>.
A versioned collection of Go packages. The go.mod file defines the module path and its dependencies.
Functions and types that work with any type using type parameters in square brackets, e.g. func Last[T any](s []T) T.
A standard interface for carrying deadlines, cancellation signals, and request-scoped values across API boundaries.
A control structure that waits on multiple channel operations. Similar to switch but for channels.
